title: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures tick up after gauges close April on record highs
body: Oops, something went wrong US stock futures ticked higher Friday morning, building on a powerful rally that pushed major indexes to fresh records and capped a standout April for equities. Futures tied to the Dow Jones Industrial Average ( YM=F ) and S&P 500 ( ES=F ) edged up 0.2% and 0.1%, respectively. Nasdaq 100 futures ( NQ=F ) hovered above the baseline. During Thursday’s regular session , stocks surged across the board. The Dow ( ^DJI ) rallied more than 790 points, or 1.6%, while the S&P 500 ( ^GSPC ) climbed just over 1% to close above 7,200 for the first time on record. The Nasdaq Composite ( ^IXIC ) added nearly 0.9% and also reached a new high. In after-hours trading, Apple ( AAPL ) jumped roughly 3% after delivering better-than-expected fiscal second-quarter results . The tech

title: Nvidia has powered one-third of the Magnificent 7's bull market gains: Chart of the Day
body: Oops, something went wrong Nvidia ( NVDA ) didn’t start this bull market — but it has come to define it. The S&P 500 ( ^GSPC ) bottomed on Oct. 12, 2022, in the wake of the 2022 bear market. But the “Magnificent Seven” tech stocks — Apple ( AAPL ), Alphabet ( GOOGL , GOOG ), Microsoft ( MSFT ), Amazon ( AMZN ), Meta ( META ), Tesla ( TSLA ), and Nvidia — didn’t fully join the party right away. The group’s index did not bottom until late December 2022 — a stretch when the S&P 500 rose 6% from its bear market low, while the Magnificent Seven fell another 12%. Then the market’s leadership changed. ChatGPT 3.5 launched on Nov. 30, 2022, and the next phase of the bull market quickly became an artificial intelligence trade, with Nvidia at the epicenter. title: Why Are Investors Ebullient About Equities but Dour Over Dividends?
body: Oops, something went wrong Since the 2015 launch of S&P 500 Annual Dividend Index Futures, there has been a persistent gap between market-implied dividend expectations and actual payouts. It’s been the same for Russell 2000 and Nasdaq 100 Dividend Index Futures since their launch in 2022. This raises the question: is the tendency to underprice realized dividend growth a harvestable risk premium or a statistical quirk? To avoid overemphasizing market pricing on any given day, we average the pricing for the annual dividend futures over the fourth quarter of each year since their launch and compare the Q4 average expectation to the actual dividend payouts in subsequent years (as shown in the charts below). The results are striking. In the vast majority of cases, realized dividend payments

title: Jane Fraser Turned Citigroup Inside Out. Now Comes the Hard Part.
body: The Fear of 13 is a new play on Broadway starring Adrien Brody about a man wrongly imprisoned for murder. title: Dow Jones Futures Rise As Apple Climbs On Earnings, Sandisk Skids; Stock Market At High
body: BREAKING: Apple Leads Earnings Movers After S&P 500, Nasdaq Hit Highs Dow Jones futures rose slightly early Friday, along with S&P 500 futures and Nasdaq futures. Apple (AAPL), Sandisk (SNDK), Western Digital (WDC) and Roku (ROKU) all beat earnings views after the close. The stock market rally had a solid session with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hitting new highs amid massive earnings news and crude oil prices pulling back.
